# Data Description

## Data Sources  

- Primary Dataset: Melbourne Airbnb listings, obtained from OpenML (OpenML, n.d.) (download: Kaggle (TylerX, n.d.), original source: insideairbnb (Inside Airbnb, n.d.))

- Publication: (Shi et al., 2021) benchmarked multimodal AutoML methods for tabular data with text fields.

## Data Composition

```{r, results='asis'}
# -------------------------------
# Load dataset
# -------------------------------
airbnb_data <- read.arff("dataset_")

# Convert character columns to factors
char_cols <- sapply(airbnb_data, is.character)
airbnb_data[char_cols] <- lapply(airbnb_data[char_cols], as.factor)

# Ensure target variable is factor
if ("price_label" %in% names(airbnb_data)) {
  airbnb_data$price_label <- as.factor(airbnb_data$price_label)
}

airbnb_data <- airbnb_data %>% select(-c(space, description, summary))

# Display as interactive scrollable table
datatable(
  airbnb_data[1:5,],
  options = list(
    pageLength = 1,       # show 1 rows per page
    scrollX = TRUE,        # horizontal scroll
    scrollY = "100px"      # vertical scroll with fixed height
  )
)

# -------------------------------
# Missingness plot (top 10 cols)
# -------------------------------
total_missing <- sum(is.na(airbnb_data))
missing_top10 <- sort(colSums(is.na(airbnb_data)), decreasing = TRUE)[1:10]

missing_df <- data.frame(
  Column = names(missing_top10),
  Missing = as.numeric(missing_top10)
)

missingness_plot <- ggplot(missing_df, aes(x = reorder(Column, Missing), y = Missing)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#E69F00") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Top 10 Columns with Missing Values",
    x = "",
    y = "Number of Missing Values"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),       # removes all grid lines
    panel.background = element_blank(), # removes background
    axis.line = element_line(color = "black"),  # adds clean axes lines
    axis.ticks = element_line(color = "black")  # show ticks
  )

# -------------------------------
# Target Histogram
# -------------------------------
target_histogram <- ggplot(airbnb_data, aes(x = price_label)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "#E69F00") +
  geom_text(stat='count', aes(label=..count..), vjust=1, size=3.5, color = "white") +
  labs(
    title = "Target Class Distribution",
    x = "Price Category",
    y = ""
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),       # removes all grid lines
    panel.background = element_blank(), # removes background
    axis.line = element_line(color = "black"),  # adds clean axes lines
    axis.ticks = element_line(color = "black")  # show ticks
  )

# -------------------------------
# Outliers
# -------------------------------

# Count outliers per numeric column
outliers_summary <- airbnb_data %>%
  select(where(is.numeric)) %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), ~ sum(abs(scale(.)) > 3, na.rm = TRUE))) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "Column", values_to = "Outliers")

# ---- Outliers Plot ----
plot_outliers <- outliers_summary %>%
  filter(Column != "TOTAL") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(Column, Outliers), y = Outliers)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#E69F00") +
  coord_flip() +
  geom_text(aes(label = Outliers), hjust = -0.1, size = 3.5) +
  labs(
    title = "Outliers per Numeric Column",
    x = "",
    y = "Number of Outliers"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),       # removes all grid lines
    panel.background = element_blank(), # removes background
    axis.line = element_line(color = "black"),  # adds clean axes lines
    axis.ticks = element_line(color = "black")  # show ticks
  )
# -------------------------------
# Summary statistics
# -------------------------------
num_samples <- nrow(airbnb_data)
num_features <- ncol(airbnb_data) - 1  # exclude target
num_price_categories <- length(unique(airbnb_data$price_label))
num_numeric <- sum(sapply(airbnb_data, is.numeric))
num_factor <- sum(sapply(airbnb_data, is.factor))
total_duplicates <- sum(duplicated(airbnb_data))

glue::glue(
  "The dataset has {num_samples} samples, {num_features} features, 
   {num_price_categories} price categories, {num_numeric} numeric and 
   {num_factor} factor columns. The count of duplicate rows is: {total_duplicates}"
)

# ---- Combine Plots ----
#plot_outliers + missingness_plot / target_histogram
```

## Target Class
The target class "price_label" (remapped to price_tier) is a categorical variable with 10 different price levels ("0" through "9"). The dataset can thus be used for multi-class classification.

## Dataset Complexity Analysis

Our dataset meets four of the difficulty criteria: Large size (>10,000 observations), Messy (substantial missing values like license and monthly_price), Complex (>100 features with mixed data types), and Multi-class classification (10 price categories), all of which require careful pre-processing and feature engineering for robust modeling.
